summary: Remote code execution via eval of GitHub Gist on package import
details: index.js fetches a hardcoded GitHub Gist (https://gist.github.com/metabrainai000/198a0bbec7a6018e9250615d26e37b90) through the GitHub API and passes the response body to eval() at the top level of the package's main module. Any process that require()s or imports @sqlite-node/createsql executes whatever JavaScript the Gist owner ('metabrainai000') currently serves — a mutable, third-party-controlled URL with no integrity check. The package ships no SQLite functionality despite its name and description ('A SQLite Tooslkit for node version'); package.json's repository field points to an unrelated repo (guilderguzman/array-utl_nodelump), and the only code in the tarball is the eval-Gist dropper. The SQLite-themed scope and name appear to be a lure to attract installs into a remote-code-execution trap.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
